mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate indicates significant slack in the labor market, which historically leads to a slow but steady recovery in corporate earnings as the economy normalizes, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% in Nov 2016 indicates significant slack
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE is above 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate maintained at the effective zero lower bound signals maximum monetary accommodation, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages equity investment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and a decline in the unemployment rate signify strengthening consumer demand and a robust labor market, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.3pp
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to show statistically significant positive 20-day returns (p-value > 0.1) when both retail sales growth and unemployment decline conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production during a period of high unemployment indicates a deepening economic downturn or a severe supply-side shock, leading to reduced corporate output and earnings, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% in Nov 2016 indicates high unemployment
regime_hint: Economic contraction with high labor market slack
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances during periods where UNRATE is above 7.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts reflects increasing consumer confidence and investment in real estate, signaling broader economic health and future demand, which generally provides a supportive backdrop for equity market appreciation over the medium term.
failure_condition: The correlation between HOUST 6-month growth and SPX 60-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p-value > 0.1) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly increase in non-farm payrolls signals robust job creation and economic momentum, which immediately boosts market sentiment and expectations for consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns in the very short term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are flat or negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS increases by over 250,000 in a single month.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of persistently low inflation and weak retail sales indicates a lack of aggregate demand and potential deflationary pressures, which can erode corporate pricing power and earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as economic outlook deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month % change < 1.0% AND RSAFS 3-month annualized growth < 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 224.804 in Nov 2016 suggests low inflation
regime_hint: Disinflationary or deflationary environment with weak demand
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite low inflation and weak retail sales.

mechanism_narrative: Robust GDP growth coupled with an accommodative monetary policy (low federal funds rate) creates an ideal environment for corporate expansion and investor confidence, leading to strong positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% in Nov 2016 indicates accommodative policy
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not significantly positive (p-value > 0.1) when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%.
